contract_summary: This function acquires a reference to an extent buffer (tree block) — either by finding an existing cached buffer or allocating a new one. It returns a pointer to an `extent_buffer` with an elevated refcount. The caller must release the reference using `free_extent_buffer()` when done. In testing mode, it uses a special allocator. If the buffer cannot be obtained (e.g., memory allocation fails), the function returns `NULL`, which the caller must check.
